candidates.stmt:
I’ve lived in Bowthorpe for over two decades and feel deeply for the community that I live in. I am standing for election in Bowthorpe as I want to offer a real, hopeful alternative to the traditional two-party system that has compassion and grassroots organisation at its core. I’ve spent my adult life working in health and social care, including at the NNUH, St Martins Housing Trust, New Routes Integration and as a frontline worker during the COVID-19 pandemic.

In 2026, life is hard for so many; expensive bills, inefficient services, inadequate mental health support, benefit cuts for the unwell and disabled, a crumbling NHS and no new social and council housing. If elected, I would like to support those struggling with the cost of living, tackle antisocial behaviour, advocate for residents regarding the future of the ward and protect and promote local green spaces, such as the Bowthorpe Marsh and Bowthorpe Southern Park.

A vote for me is a vote for a local girl who genuinely cares about those around her and who will always call out corruption, collusion and injustice.

I’m standing for Reform UK because I want to leave a better country and a better future for my children. This expectation that the next generation should inherit a better world from the previous generation has in the past been a given but now it is very much in doubt. The Conservatives, Labour and the Liberal Democrats have all presided over nearly three decades of managed decline of our economy, rapid change to our society and a sustained attack on our values.

Britain deserves much better and Reform UK are the only party offering real change and hope for a better future. The three legacy parties all believe in mass immigration, big government, high taxes, expensive energy and the woke agenda. Reform UK are the only party to reject this orthodoxy.

I have voted for the Conservatives in ten general elections but never again. Ideologically my position hasn’t changed over the years but the Tories have abandoned me and moved to the left, embracing globalist, corporatist values that I don’t align with. We need to put our country and its people first and that’s what Reform UK are proposing.

I have lived in Norwich for 23 years, I have raised my family here and started a small business here. Norfolk needs a fresh mandate in order to end decades of financial mismanagement. Tax payers deserve better services and better value for money from their local authority. Reform UK will run the council for the benefit of the people who pay for it rather than the people who work for it.

I have always liked to give something back to my community whenever I can. Since joining the Liberal Democrats I have been encouraged to engage in local politics, campaigning to improve my local area and the lives of fellow residents.

Voter participation and engaged democracy are things I care for passionately and have worked to improve all of my adult life, particularly amongst younger voters. Whilst campaigning for Remain in the EU referendum, I spoke and took part in events motivating voters to sign up and make their voices heard and I believe that the public should be given the opportunity to have a vote on the final Brexit deal.

I am also passionate about the environment, as a member of several organisations and volunteering with conservation groups.

Born in Norwich, I live locally in Sewell, and work in the city centre.

I would like to serve Catton Grove as it’s Councillor (City/County) by:
• Attend meetings to put forward your views and vote for resolutions 
• Take local issues seriously and do my upmost to resolve
• I would look to provide the best services as possible whilst being as efficient as possible
• I will work hard to try and reduce the 900 million pounds debt which currently incurs 200 million pounds interest per year. Just to put that in context it’s around 100, 000 households paying council tax to pay the interests, and when you factor in loan payments it’s probably nearly 50% of Norfolks households paying for the debt, which means around 50% of households are paying for 100% of the services ( This is only an estimate as I do not know what the payments are on the loan balance) - either way it’s an awful situation to be in

Tony has lived in Norwich since 2009 and has been a member of the Green Party for nearly 30 years. He has previously been a Green Party councillor in Dereham, having been elected to Dereham Town Council three times and to Breckland District Council for one four-year term.

Tony ran a wholefood shop in Dereham for 17 years until 2002. He was chairman of Dereham Chamber of Trade for two years. He also co-wrote the proposal that brought the local “Nipper” bus service to Dereham. He now runs an organic veg-box scheme based here in Norwich.

He has chosen to stand to represent the Green Party because he feels impressed by the caring and responsive manner with which it tackles issues facing our community today. He is particularly interested in supporting local businesses and shops to help make our local community more vibrant. He believes that the old saying ‘think global, act local’ is still relevant today and his actions are a testament to that.

He has been pleased to champion a more holistic, sustainable way of life and would be proud to represent the Green Party again on a local council.

When not campaigning, Tony can be found playing table tennis, performing music, or out trying to learn birdsongs on the meadows by the River Wensum.

Our Labour priorities for Crome

1.	We will work with colleagues across Norwich City Council to support low-income and no income people who have kept our country running while many of us were in lockdown or isolating at home.

2.	We will take action to improve air quality both inside and outside our schools, working with local schools to educate children and parents about what they can do to help. We will continue to protect and cultivate our open, green spaces.

3.	We will fight to improve outcomes for young children and young people

4.	We will continue work with the safer neighbourhood police team and other agencies to combat anti-social behaviour and County Lines drug dealing, aim to secure more CCTV, and work with Norwich City Council to tackle the causes of crime. We will work to improve the lives of vulnerable women via community safety structures across the city and county.

5.	We are pleased for the community that our campaign for a 20-mph speed in 5 roads including Wolfe Road is successful, following Norfolk County Council’s response to our question to them that it is appropriate - and our fight for further speed limits in residential areas will continue.

As a council tenant here in Lakenham, I see what you see every day, I know it’s time for a change. 
I’m running to get the basics right: cleaning up our streets, ending fly-tipping, and ensuring our children have safe, clean places to play. I understand the frustration of waiting for the system to work, and I will be the voice that finally demands Lakenham’s fair share.

​For too long, we’ve been represented by people who don't live our reality. I am committed to fighting for the high-quality social housing and reliable services we deserve. 
If elected I will to take our shared experience to the Council to ensure no resident feels disenfranchised or ignored again.

1. **​Practical Politics** : I believe in common-sense solutions that make our lives easier—better maintenance, warmer homes, and cleaner streets.

​ 2.**Working for You**: I’m ready to put the hard graft in. You deserve a councillor who is visible, reachable, and understands the value of a pound.

​ 3.**A Green Future for All**: Going Green shouldn't be a luxury for the wealthy. It should mean lower bills and better living standards for everyone in our community.

​Lakenham doesn't need more empty promises. It needs a councillor who knows the reality of life in social housing and is willing to work for it.

I’m Richard “Dicky” Eminson. Im a semi retired airline steward, that worked on the airlines for eleven years. Born in Southport, on a horticultural farm. I had a strong work ethic drilled into me from around 8 years old, as I helped my father work the land, producing seasonal crops.

After obtaining a business degree at Lancs uni. I moved directly to London. After various jobs, which included 5 years as a floor manager in Harrods. I eventually settled in the East End of London with my partner. After a friend of mine, who was cabin crew, found that easyJet was recruiting. I found my dream job! I am the ultimate people person that thrive on hospitality.

Activities outside these work scenarios, I have volunteered on a befriending course for Crisis (the homeless charity). I have raised over £10,000 for The Anthony Nolan Trust, via two London marathons. As well as many fun runs for Cancer charities.

After living in Brighton for 7 years and running my own business. I came to Norfolk for a short break over a year ago and simply fell in love with the people  and relaxed vide. I reside in Central Norwich, so very accessible. I class myself as likeable and approachable.  And more importantly I try and be as supportive as I can to any community I reside.

Dr Gurpreet Padda (she/her) has today 20th March 2024 been selected as parliamentary candidate for Waveney Valley.



As an environmental scientist, Gurpreet is committed to working hard in the area of environmental science and conservation. She has worked at the Centre for Environment Fisheries and Aquaculture Science (Cefas) lab in Lowestoft and is working in government, and completed her PhD in Environmental Sciences at the University of East Anglia. Gurpreet is founder of Black Ocean Citizens, a non-profit aimed at connecting diverse and underrepresented communities to water-based and ocean sciences. As well as working in Norwich, she also serves Lakenham ward as Norwich City Councillor and is involved in decisions concerning the provision of affordable housing, tackling crime, equality & diversity, climate change and creating opportunities for local training and employment.



With ties across Waveney Valley, Norfolk and Suffolk, Gurpreet lives in Norwich with her husband, two children and Luna the dog. Daughter of first generation immigrants, Gurpreet understands the value of hard work and being part of a community. She enjoys listening to music, spending time with her family and friends outdoors and on the river, fishing.



Gurpreet says "I am aware of challenges facing the Waveney Valley community including poor services as a result of dwindling public finance and not having enough low carbon affordable homes to support growing local families. With Labour marked as the succeeding party, no other candidate can and will ensure local voices are heard in Parliament."



"I am excited to be representing Waveney Valley. With Labour in government, I want to be a strong advocate for local people in Westminster. Issues that are important to me are:



Cleaner -- As part of Labour's Clean Energy goals, hold water companies to account to ensure Good Environmental Status is achieved regarding the health of River Waveney

Greener -- With Labour's Green Prosperity and Gr

Sean is a local writer and communications specialist who has lived in Norwich for almost a decade. Based in Bowthorpe since coming to Norwich to attend the UEA, he has been a proud and active member of our fine city’s community from the very start. He is thrilled to be standing to represent the people of South Norwich as their MP and looks forward to speaking to many of them over the course of the campaign.

Denise has represented Nelson ward for the past eight years. She wants to continue to represent local residents and stand up for her community. Denise has successfully campaigned for a 20mph speed limit on a number of Nelson streets and plans to continue this campaign until the whole ward has a 20mph limit. Denise has also fought for the right of residents to maintain grass tennis courts at Heigham Park and for a community group to look after the courts. She believes that the city council should use the money it plans to spend installing more modern tennis courts should be spent, instead, on maintaining children's playgrounds in other parts of the city.

Denise has been a staunch supporter of local shops at Earlham House, dealing with a number of issues to do with the building and its environs over the years. She is hoping that recent government legislation can be used to make the company that has been handing out harsh fines to shoppers act more responsibly.

On a city-wide level, Denise is keen to see Norwich take its fair share of action in the battle against climate change, consistent with the Paris Agreement. She has proposed motions to the city council to create a strategy to adapt to the effects climate change and to declare a climate emergency. Her work in this area has been very important. There is still much to be done to persuade the Labour administration of the importance of these issues.

Denise's background in planning and transport has been useful in her life as a city councillor. She has served on the council's Scrutiny and Planning committees, as well as committees dealing with transport and planning issues, Norwich Highways Agency Committee and the Sustainable Development Panel. She is currently the Leader of the Green Group, and the Leader of the Opposition on the city council.

Denise has lived in Nelson ward for 35 years, along with her husband and their cats.

Having lived and grown up in Sewell Ward- I know what it is like to be a child, young person, and adult in this area and I know first hand what needs to be done for our community to thrive. I’d like to focus on loneliness and isolation, litter, flytipping and keeping our area safe and clean for everyone to enjoy.

If the voters of Thorpe Hamlet place their confidence in me, I will:

1. Fight for adequate social housing and scrutinise developments to make sure they benefit the whole community, not just a handful of landlords.

2. Demand that the rights of the river and the people and wildlife it supports be upheld, holding polluters to account.

3. Ensure that green spaces are clean and parks and playgrounds are fit for purpose.

4. Push for safer streets.

Dear Resident,
I've lived in Norwich for over forty years where I have worked in the print trade, served as a police officer and now in the NHS. My children have grown up here and I consider Norwich to be a fine city but, it could be so much better. Years of mismanagement under Labour and Conservatives have left us millions of pounds in debt due to complacency and poor decision making. We in Reform UK believe the tax paying residents should be central to any spending plans. Value for money, accountability and transparency in local government must be central to everything we do. delivering better value for money and stimulating the local economy will be my focus. 
We all know Britain is broken and needs reforming. Vote Reform Uk on May 7th and together let's start that process. Thank you

I have represented Town Close Ward since 2019 and am proud to work closely with Green and Independent colleagues. Norwich is a fantastic place to be - we're big enough to enjoy city life, but small enough that you can know your neighbourhood. My guiding principles are climate justice and social justice and a firm belief that community, as well as government, should help take care of us all. When the basics are covered, people have time and space to enjoy life, and there's no good reason why that's not possible!

I retired recently from working as a laboratory technician at the Quadram Institute Bioscience. This has given me the free time to become more involved in community concerns. I have lived at my present address, in the ward, for over 25 years and have formerly been a City Councillor.

Traffic congestion and parking problems are a big concern for me. I think much more use could be made of the Park and Ride service so that it becomes commonplace to get a bus into the city.

There also needs to be a way to mitigate the impact of student houses in Nelson, as overflowing letter boxes and bins left in the street are upsetting for long term residents.

It annoys me when contractors dig a hole in the pavement, put barriers round it, then nothing gets done for days or weeks to finish the job, as has happened many times locally. Such disruption must be minimised.

**About me**
Hi! I live in Wensum, never done politics before so I'm stepping up to try and make a difference. I have a keen interest in politics and I've always paid attention to environmental issues. I've leafleted Wensum Ward for the Green Party, chatted to residents about their concerns, and I was impressed with Zack Polanski’s politics so I joined the Green Party in 2025.

I came to Norwich in 1998 and have lived in Wensum Ward for 5 years. Now I have a family I’m far more aware of how essential local community is to all our lives. I’ve been widely involved in community projects over the years, from theatre to anti-fascist activism, and have recently become a volunteer at the Wensum Community Centre.

**What I can bring?**
I am a good organiser with experience coordinating groups of volunteers. For 10 years I worked in Film & TV production, I've also worked in museum and educational settings, utilising my arts and acting experience to engage students.

My priorities as a city councillor for Wensum would be:

- Housing: I'm passionate about engaging with local residents to learn their needs and housing is often a top priority. 
- Green spaces: keeping parks tidy and well maintained, but also the smaller spaces that we see and use every day - footpaths, hedges, grass verges. Fly tipping is a constant issue round here and much more needs to be done to deter the culprits. 
- Wellbeing: I think talking to people who live near you is the best cure for nasty, divisive right wing politics. I want to support and develop resources like our community centres, where residents can come together to feel happy and safer.

I have ADHD; it's not a superpower, most days it makes my life difficult, but I'm an effective problem solver when I lock on to something (please bring snacks!)

**Why vote for me?**
I'm interested in what people care about and why. I'm ready to listen but I will speak up if I disagree and explain why. I think we owe it to one another to be honest - with oth

Ben has lived in Norwich since moving to the city in 2001. A gardener by profession and father of two children, he has been a Norwich city councillor since 2012 and a Norfolk county councillor since 2021.

Ben is standing for both the county and city seats in Wensum because he wants to help bring real positive change to local residents, and feels that both the Labour and Conservative administrations have simply failed to deliver.

Ben has a good understanding of the issues that matter to the people of Norwich. He has worked alongside residents to solve local problems, while trying to effect positive change across the city.

Ben acutely understands the importance of biodiversity and the impacts climate change is having on the natural and human worlds

He brings extensive experience in governance having served on the county council’s Strategic and Corporate Select Committee, and chaired the city council’s Audit Committee with distinction.

As the Green Group’s lead on finance at County Hall, Ben has consistently proposed budget amendments that prioritise the community’s future—reallocating funds from the costly road building schemes, toward better buses, safer cycling, and walking infrastructure.

His practical expertise also drives his push for more street trees, natural flood defenses, and a reduction in pesticide use.

If re-elected, Ben is committed to championing "wrap-around" care, with a particular focus on improving mental health services and holistic support to ensure that any residents that need suport, will receive the compassionate care they deserve.

Ben will also fight tirelessly for Norwich to be a leader in transitioning to a low-carbon economy. Helping to attract high quality jobs and significant investment to the city.

Having gone from studying Law at the University of East Anglia to training at a Norwich City firm and rising to partner level, before founding Gordon Dean Solicitors, my personal and professional life has been consistently intertwined with Norwich.

With an office on Princes Street and a home near the city centre I have enormous pride, in my family, my staff and my business, comparison to which, gives some humility.

I have previously been a County Councillor and am on board of Norfolk Community Law Service (NCLS), which provides free advice on employment, family, debt, housing and discrimination law.

Passionately opposed to Brexit and Homelessness.
